title: [release-v1.14] CI in_podman: don't get tripped up by CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

In CI, we don't handle passing environment values that contain whitespace through to tests that we run inside of podman, so we need to screen out $CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE.

#### How to verify it

Check if the `in_podman` task passes for this PR in CI, give or take other problems we have in this branch.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Cherry picked from #2553.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM
but tests are not happy.
@nalind lint issue, and I could have sworn we'd made those changes to .cirrus.yml in prior run throughs...
@TomSweeneyRedHat I hadn't pulled in changes from `.golangci.yml` yet, giving that a go.

@nalind, I just remembered, we had worked through these, but then my PR got closed and @cevich has been struggling with it in #2493.  We may want to punt, just to do the title change then ask @edsantiago to run the tests against it "by hand".

> and @cevich has been struggling with it in #2493 

There's a reasonable possibility I won't get to that PR this sprint or next.  IIRC, it was involved-enough of a problem I'd really need to drop-everything and focus on it.  Though if @edsantiago wants to peek/poke at the problem (given the changes here), another pair of eyes (especially his) may reveal the problem is simpler than I've built it up to be.

Anyhoo, I peeped at the changes here and they seem okay.  I remember the "envars passing into container args" thing being sticky for me.  For context, it was another PAPR/legacy workaroud item and I was in a rush.  There's no doubt some gremlin-WTFs burred in the translation.

At this point, the failures we're seeing are on Ubuntu, and both are [`container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "error adding seccomp rule for syscall socket: requested action matches default action of filter`](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6348336271720448?command=integration_test#L1020).

ISTR @mheon and @rhatdan seeing similar problems yesterday in the #podman channel on IRC.  Hopefully they can shed some light on this, but either way I'd be okay with chasing it down in a different PR, since that error doesn't look to me like it's specific to our CI environment.

In short, runc does not work with the current Seccomp profile from https://github.com/seccomp/containers-golang which is somehow being used in those images. You need an older version of the Seccomp profile without the conditional Socket call.

Right, that problem.  So IIRC @baude was waiting for @lsm5 to build new rpm w/ fixed seccomp, then we can build new VM images with that, then we can reference the new images across the repos hitting the problem.  Whew!

> Right, that problem. So IIRC @baude was waiting for @lsm5 to build new rpm w/ fixed seccomp, then we can build new VM images with that, then we can reference the new images across the repos hitting the problem. Whew!

umm, yesterday @baude told me we were good to go. Was there a ping that I missed?

I'm told what's needed is:
Ref: https://github.com/seccomp/containers-golang/pull/39

Once that gets packaged up, we'll get new VM images built and then those can be incorporated here to fix the issue.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-v1.14/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@nalind do we still need this or can we close it?
The "cron" invocations in CI are still failing for this branch, so there's work to be done, yes.

> "cron" invocations in CI are still failing for this branch

Thanks for keeping an eye on that @nalind .  I'm sorry I haven't been able to be more attentive :disappointed:
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@nalind @cevich is this still something we care about?
I think this can probably safely be closed, it would likely be a painful rebase at best.
